title: The nephron
body_html:
The nephron is the functional unit of the kidney. There are 2 basic types of nephron:
  • Cortical nephrons, comprise ~85% of the total number of nephrons
  • Juxtamedullary nephrons, comprise ~15% of the total number of nephrons

Cortical nephrons have glomeruli in the outer 70% of the cortex and have short loops of Henle whereas juxtamedullary nephrons have glomeruli close to the cortex-medullary boundary and long loops of Henle.

The basic structure of the nephron is shown below.

The nephron. click on image to enlarge

Cortical vs juxtamedullary nephrons. click on image to enlarge

The first component of the nephron is Bowman's capsule which surrounds glomerular capillaries and collects the filtrate that passes through them. Bowman's capsule and the glomerulus are collectively known as the renal corpuscle.

Once collected by the Bowman's capsule plasma filtrate moves into the proximal convoluted tubule (PCT) before entering the loop of Henle. Filtrate moves from the ascending loop of Henle into the distal convoluted tubule (DCT) and eventually passes via a connecting tubule into the collecting duct. Note the collecting duct is not considered part of the nephron. The PCT, loop of Henle and DCT are collectively referred to as the renal tubule.

Functions of nephron components

ComponentFunction
Bowman's capsuleCollect filtrate generated by the glomerulus
PCTResorption of water and solutes
Secretion of drugs and toxins
Loop of HenleReabsorption of water
Concentration of urine
DCTTubuloglomerular feedback via the macula densa
Resorption of ions
Acid-base control
2
